programmes.show_description: Pianist Alexander Malofeev joins the German Symphony Orchestra, Berlin and conductor Elim Chan in Rachmaninov's 2nd Piano Concerto and Prokofiev's Symphony no 5. John Shea presents.
John Toal presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Ian Skelly plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
Linton Stephens introduces an afternoon of specially made recordings, including sacred music by Bach and Haydn and recent chamber music recordings from the Crucible, Sheffield.
Live from Newcastle Cathedral for the Wednesday in Holy Week, with music by Gesualdo, Eriks Esenvalds, Dvorák and Dupré.
...of Bingen and Isabella Leonarda. 3/5. Hildegard becomes a leader and strikes out on her own. With Donald Macleod.
Katie Derham presents live music from pianist Libby Burgess, cellist Cara Berridge and violinist Sophie Rosa. Katie also interviews the directors of the new QueerScore festival.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical music.
Alina Ibragimova is the soloist in Bartók's Violin Concerto No. 1, and Edward Gardner conducts the London Philharmonic Orchestra in Mussorgsky's Pictures at an Exhibition.

programmes.show_name: The Essay: The Lives of Hildegard of Bingen
programmes.show_description: 3/5. Medieval composer and polymath Hildegard of Bingen navigates challenges as a prioress. A fictionalised account, written by Anna Linstrum and performed by Juliet Stevenson.
Sara Mohr-Pietsch presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Tonight on 'Round Midnight, Thai drummer Salin returns to share her third 4/4 pick of the week, and Soweto spins a new release from the genre-blending ensemble DREAMSCAPES.
Organist Pavao Masic joins the Croatian Radiotelevision Symphony Orchestra and conductor Pascal Rophé in Saint-Saëns' 'Organ' Symphony, plus a premiere by Kresimir Seletkovic.
John Toal presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Ian Skelly plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
Linton Stephens introduces an afternoon of specially made recordings, including Górecki's Symphony No. 3 and recent chamber music recordings from the Crucible, Sheffield.
...of Bingen and Isabella Leonarda. 4/5. Donald Macleod examines how Leonarda's music gained a wider audience beyond the walls of her convent.
Katie Derham is joined live in studio for a performance by a newly announced New Generation Artist.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical music from your favourite composers such as Ravel, Debussy and Evelyn Glennie.
Tenebrae joins the Britten Sinfonia for an emotionally charged evening, culminating in James MacMillan's Passion meditation, Seven Last Words from the Cross.

programmes.show_name: The Essay: The Lives of Hildegard of Bingen
programmes.show_description: 4/5. Medieval composer and polymath Hildegard of Bingen builds her own community but experiences a great loss. Written by Anna Linstrum and performed by Juliet Stevenson.
Sara Mohr-Pietsch presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Salin is back with her fourth and final 4/4 pick of the week and Soweto has more of the best new jazz releases.
